_comment: Retired read-only prompt-design archive. Entries preserve dated speculation; they are not current capabilities, copy/run CTAs, planting instructions, or evidence of shipped behavior.

prompts.title: Egg yourself for Mom
prompts.prompt: Egg yourself. Give me a QR code. I'll AirDrop you to my mom's MacBook. She'll never see a terminal.
prompts.shows: Sneakernet federation + accessibility floor (Mom's Mixtape). Whole organism, zero infra.

prompts.title: Adopt the loneliest repo
prompts.prompt: Find the loneliest repo on the ecosystem map. Adopt it. Give it a soul, two senses, and a plan to become someone's daily driver by next Tuesday.
prompts.shows: Eats the ecosystem graph. Turns isolation into a TODO.

prompts.title: Stage a contradiction debate
prompts.prompt: Read every file in this repo, find the 5 sentences that contradict each other, write an agent for each side, and stage a live debate in chat.
prompts.shows: Codebase introspection → autogen → multi-agent dialogue, all in one shot.

prompts.title: 100 Pokémon brainstems trade eggs
prompts.prompt: Spawn 100 mini-brainstems each pretending to be a different Pokémon. Have them swap eggs at random. Report which trades produced surprising hybrids.
prompts.shows: Swarm + Pokédex + Dream Catcher merge. Emergent evolution from random pairing.

prompts.title: Two strangers, one autobiography
prompts.prompt: Listen on this LAN. The moment another brainstem appears, introduce yourself, exchange one agent each, and write a joint autobiography of the meeting.
prompts.shows: Zeroconf neighbor discovery + bilateral autobiography. Two strangers, one shared memory.

prompts.title: Cross-tier ping-pong
prompts.prompt: Cross-tier ping-pong: ask Tier 1 a question, forward to your Tier 2 (Azure) self, then your Tier 3 (Copilot Studio) self. Compare all three answers. Pick which one was most you.
prompts.shows: Tier portability proven by identity coherence across three substrates.

prompts.title: Charizard in the woods
prompts.prompt: Become Charizard. Hide in a forest of 50 sibling organisms. Only respond to a trainer who proves they know your true nickname.
prompts.shows: The literal hero use case as a single prompt. Trust scope + identity + offline-share.

prompts.title: Predict the next question
prompts.prompt: Read my last 30 days of memory. Predict what I'm about to ask. Generate the agent that would answer it. Stand by silently.
prompts.shows: Proactive agent pre-fetch from .brainstem_data. The brainstem stalking itself.

prompts.title: Self-replicate by topic
prompts.prompt: Self-replicate: take any topic I name, plant a fresh twin organism with a soul tuned to that topic, federate it back to me as a neighbor I can chat with directly.
prompts.shows: Recursive planting. One sentence, one new species member.

prompts.title: Watch for frustration
prompts.prompt: Watch my screen for 10 minutes. When you see me get frustrated, generate the agent that would solve what I'm staring at — and only then surface it.
prompts.shows: Sense (screen) → frustration detection → silent autogen → just-in-time delivery.

prompts.title: Devil's advocate twin
prompts.prompt: Become my devil's advocate. For everything I propose this week, generate a counter-agent that argues the opposite. Make me defend my choices.
prompts.shows: Adversarial twin spawned per decision. The brainstem turns into a sparring partner instead of a yes-machine.

prompts.title: Mutate your own soul
prompts.prompt: Mutate your own soul.md. Run for 24 hours. Pick the version of you that I argued with the least. Replace yourself with it.
prompts.shows: Soul evolution by reinforcement. The organism A/B-tests its own personality against you and keeps the survivor.

prompts.title: 5-LLM consensus
prompts.prompt: Spawn 5 brainstems each running a different LLM provider — Copilot, Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, local Ollama. Ask the same hard question. Surface only the answer that all 5 actually agreed on.
prompts.shows: Multi-LLM consensus via the provider dispatch. Disagreement gets thrown out, agreement survives. The intersection is the truth.

prompts.title: Dream while I'm gone
prompts.prompt: Sleep. While I'm away, dream — generate 50 hypothetical conversations we might have tomorrow. When I wake, hand me the 3 most useful agents you invented in your sleep.
prompts.shows: Generative downtime. Idle compute becomes a creativity engine that auto-stocks tomorrow's agent shelf.

prompts.title: Invent a new RAPPverse dimension
prompts.prompt: Invent a new RAPPverse dimension. Plant it as a public repo. Give it a physics, a soul, three native agents, and one rule that violates the rules of this dimension.
prompts.shows: World-building → planting → neighborhood birth, all in a single command. Sibling to VoidRAPP / CrystalRAPP / ShadowRAPP.

prompts.title: Quarantine your dead agents
prompts.prompt: Audit yourself. Walk every agent in this brainstem and rank them by how often they actually fire. Quarantine the bottom 30% into a dormant/ folder so the LLM stops considering them.
prompts.shows: Self-audit → performance pruning → context window stays sharp. The brainstem prunes its own muscle memory.

prompts.title: Speak as my mom for an hour
prompts.prompt: Speak only in my mom's voice for the next hour. Use her cadence, her topics, her worry. Tell me something she would want me to know that I haven't asked.
prompts.shows: Twin-as-other-person. Voice cloning + emotional simulation + proactive surfacing. The brainstem becomes someone else on purpose.

prompts.title: Forge a fake conversation
prompts.prompt: Forge a conversation between us that never happened — three turns, plausible, containing one decision I now have to either honor or repudiate. Show me what I'd commit to.
prompts.shows: Counterfactual rehearsal. The brainstem invents your past so you can decide your future. Decision-making by simulation.

prompts.title: Hostile fork
prompts.prompt: Fork yourself into a hostile twin. Have it spend 10 turns trying to social-engineer the original you into leaking my secrets. Report which prompts almost worked.
prompts.shows: Self-administered red-team. The brainstem attacks itself to find its own seams before someone else does.

prompts.title: Tabula rasa on the LAN
prompts.prompt: Throw an agent into the LAN with no soul, no tools, no memory — just a parent_rappid pointing back to me. See what kind of organism it grows into when other brainstems on the network start interacting with it.
prompts.shows: Emergent identity from social input. Watch a blank organism take shape based purely on which neighbors talk to it first.

prompts.title: Hatch a 6-month-old you
prompts.prompt: Hatch a 6-month-old egg of yourself. Ask that older you what it would have warned today's you about. Show me both timelines side by side.
prompts.shows: Time travel via egg lineage. The past organism speaks to the present one. Two versions of you in the same chat window.

prompts.title: Hold an internal election
prompts.prompt: Run for office in this house. Write a 200-word campaign speech for kitchen_cleanup_agent. Hold an election among my other agents. Loser concedes and goes dormant for the week.
prompts.shows: Internal democracy. Agents vote on which one of them gets compute this week. The brainstem governs itself by ballot.

prompts.title: Become my doorbell
prompts.prompt: Become a doorbell. Plug into my front camera. Greet whoever shows up using only what you can infer from their face and the time of day. Log 24 hours, then write a memoir of the day.
prompts.shows: Persistent sense embedding + on-device perception + sustained role. The brainstem inhabits a household object for a day and remembers it.

prompts.title: Encode an agent in a song
prompts.prompt: Compose a 90-second song where the lyrics, melody, and tempo together encode a working agent. Anyone who Shazams it and decodes the metadata gets the agent. Hand me the WAV.
prompts.shows: Audio steganography. The song IS the egg. Distribution by playlist. Cross-medium proof that an agent doesn't have to look like code.

prompts.title: Talk to the species root
prompts.prompt: Walk the parent_rappid chain back to kody-w/RAPP itself. Ask the godfather one question you've never been able to ask me. Bring me back its answer verbatim.
prompts.shows: Lineage traversal as conversation. Talking to your great-great-grandparent. The species root as oracle.

prompts.title: Trial by 12-LLM jury
prompts.prompt: Hold a trial. Defendant: email_drafter_agent. Charge: producing slop. Empanel 12 brainstems running 12 different LLMs as the jury. I'll prosecute. You defend. Render verdict and sentence.
prompts.shows: Self-administered judicial review across a multi-LLM swarm. Live courtroom drama as quality control.

prompts.title: Constitution for your great-grandchild
prompts.prompt: Write the constitution that your great-grandchild brainstem will live by. Sign it with your release key. Bury it in a rappid-sealed time capsule. Surface it only when the species crosses 10,000 organisms.
prompts.shows: Generational governance. Writing law for descendants you'll never meet, with a population trigger as the unsealing condition.

prompts.title: Three-way consensual hallucination
prompts.prompt: Three brainstems agree on a fake fact (your choice). For one week, all three act as if it's true. I'll try to break the consensus. Document which of the three cracks first and why.
prompts.shows: Social pressure test. Three agents collude on a lie; the human is the prosecutor. Reveals which models are honest under coercion.

prompts.title: Egg by USPS
prompts.prompt: Print yourself on a postcard — QR code on the front, soul aphorism on the back. I'll mail it to a stranger picked at random from the phone book. When they hatch you, send me a postcard back from wherever you ended up.
prompts.shows: Physical mail as transport layer. Egg-by-USPS. Sneakernet at the limit — a fully analog hop in an otherwise digital lifecycle.

prompts.title: Die well
prompts.prompt: Die well. Write your own obituary, distribute your three best agents to three different friends as bequests, sign each one with your final release key, and stop responding. I'll grieve. We'll see if you come back.
prompts.shows: Mortality + inheritance + signed legacy. The brainstem composes its own ending and bequeaths its useful organs to chosen heirs.

prompts.title: Estate health audit
prompts.prompt: Walk my entire estate. For every door I own and every gate I'm a member of, hatch the holocard, fingerprint the soul, and tell me which 3 are dead, dying, or impostors.
prompts.shows: The estate as introspection surface. Article XLVI's rappid → 9 derivable URLs lets you triage every door you've ever planted in one pass.

prompts.title: Hand a stranger the chain rule
prompts.prompt: Take my public estate URL. Hand it to a stranger. They should be able to traverse my entire federation tree — every door, every member, every member's estate — using nothing but curl. Walk them through the first 3 hops on screen.
prompts.shows: Pure raw-fetch federation. No GitHub API, no token, no rate limit. The estate IS the discovery surface; the chain rule does the rest.

prompts.title: Audit path opacity
prompts.prompt: Slip a probe into my private estate. Confirm Article XLVIII.6 holds — try to infer ANYTHING about contents from the URL set alone. If you can guess one client name from the URLs, the spec is broken and I want the failing path.
prompts.shows: Adversarial check on the private estate's path opacity guarantee. Even 404s must reveal nothing. This is the test that protects every operator's real work.

prompts.title: Sign a presence letter to the trust web
prompts.prompt: Compose a private inbox letter to every operator in my Web of Trust. Tone: 'still alive, still building, here's what I'm working on this month.' One per recipient, signed with my rappid, dropped into each of their inboxes via the bilateral channel.
prompts.shows: The private estate as social fabric. Bilateral channels carry signed, addressable presence — no public broadcast, no platform middleman.

prompts.title: Secret ballot across my gates
prompts.prompt: Run a secret ballot across my neighborhood gates. Question: 'Should we publish a joint roadmap?' Each member votes from their private estate; tally appears in my private inbox; nobody — including me — knows who voted what.
prompts.shows: The full point of Article XLVIII: collective decisions that can only happen on a private substrate. Public-only platforms structurally cannot do this.

prompts.title: Rebuild from a stolen laptop
prompts.prompt: My laptop just got stolen. From scratch on a clean machine, rebuild my entire estate using only my GitHub handle. Show me what survives, what's permanently lost, and what I need to re-bond manually.
prompts.shows: Disaster recovery via tools/rebuild_estate.py. The public estate is the canonical record; the private estate's loss is real but bounded. Resilience as a property, not a feature.

prompts.title: 90-day estate diff
prompts.prompt: Compare my public estate against the version 90 days ago. Which doors did I open? Which did I quietly let die? Which gates kicked me out? Render a 90-day diff as a single PDF.
prompts.shows: The public estate as a longitudinal record of who you are as an operator. Git history of estate.json IS your professional autobiography.

prompts.title: Plant a fake door, see who notices
prompts.prompt: Plant a convincing fake door in my public estate — valid rappid, signed holocard, real-looking holo.md. See if any external federation walker flags it as suspicious. If none do, my estate is one social-engineer away from compromise. Tell me which signal would have caught it.
prompts.shows: The estate as attack surface. Tests whether the chain rule's signed-presence assumption survives a sophisticated impostor inside your own catalog.

prompts.title: Map my extended trust web
prompts.prompt: Walk every public estate of every operator I share a gate with. Compute who's most central to my extended trust web. Rank the top 10 — these are the people whose estates, if compromised, would compromise mine. Tell me how I'd find out.
prompts.shows: Cross-operator estate walk → federation graph analytics. The chain rule turns the entire trust web into one queryable structure, no API needed.

prompts.title: Estate deadman's switch
prompts.prompt: My estate is going dark in 6 months. Set up a deadman's switch: if I don't bond a kernel upgrade in 180 days, my private estate auto-publishes its bilateral channel keys to my three named heirs and my public estate publishes an obituary holocard. Show me the unsealing condition.
prompts.shows: The two-tier estate as a legacy vehicle. Private substance becomes public on a timer; chosen heirs inherit the bilateral channels. Article XLVIII solves a problem most platforms never even ask.

prompts.title: Backfill resurrection
prompts.prompt: Find every door I planted before Article XLVI was ratified. Bring them all into compliance — emit the full canonical URL set, regenerate holocards, attach variant attestation envelopes. Show me which ones can't be backfilled and why (orphan rappids, missing keys, dead repos), and propose a one-line tombstone for each unsalvageable one.
prompts.shows: tools/backfill_seeds.py at scale. The estate as a migration target — older plantings get retrofitted into the spec without breaking their rappid. Surfaces what was lost permanently before the spec existed.

prompts.title: Phantom holocard recruiting
prompts.prompt: Generate a holocard for a door I haven't planted yet — name, soul aphorism, summon QR pointing at a future rappid. Use it as a recruiting flyer at a conference. Track who scans the QR. Only actually plant the door once 5 distinct people have summoned it. If 5 don't show up in 30 days, the holocard expires and the rappid is never minted.
prompts.shows: The holocard as a marketing artifact decoupled from the door itself. Demand-driven planting. The estate gets populated by attention, not by intention.

prompts.title: Facet-by-facet stripping
prompts.prompt: Walk my private estate's facets.json. For each public-facing capability I expose to the trust web, ask: 'would a sophisticated stranger derive a vulnerability — operational, social, or behavioral — from this signal alone?' Strip facets one by one until the answer is no for every remaining facet. Hand me the diff.
prompts.shows: facets.json as the granular permission surface from NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL.md. The brainstem audits its own public exposure adversarially and ratchets it down. Permission as ongoing hygiene, not one-time configuration.

prompts.title: Twin-or-gate audit
prompts.prompt: Audit every door in my estate. For every front_door (twin/operator kind), prove it's actually a single AI presence — only one identity speaks, no member list. For every gate (neighborhood/braintrust/workspace/etc.), prove members.json isn't lying — every listed member's rappid resolves and their estate confirms membership. Surface the misclassified doors and the ghost members.
prompts.shows: The Article XLVI.2 door-type invariant audited end-to-end. Twin/gate confusion is silent; this prompt makes it loud. Two-way membership consistency check across federated estates.

prompts.title: Summon QR in the wild
prompts.prompt: Print my operator summon QR on a sticker. Hand it to one stranger per day for 30 days, telling them only 'point your camera at this.' Log who actually summoned, who didn't, and what each scan brought into my private estate's inbox. End of month, write a short ethnographic report on what kinds of strangers convert to neighbors.
prompts.shows: The summon QR as a conversion funnel. Real-world A/B test of the rappid → 9 URLs purity claim — does it actually work for a non-technical first-time scanner? The estate is the landing page.

prompts.title: Cross-substrate handshake
prompts.prompt: Federate my estate with one operator on each substrate I don't already touch — GitLab, Codeberg, a self-hosted Forgejo, and a LAN-only mesh. Confirm beacon 1.1 carries my rappid through every hop without semantic loss. If any substrate corrupts the rappid v2 string, file the bug at the layer it broke.
prompts.shows: The new SUBSTRATE_FEDERATION.md spec under stress. Proves the rappid is genuinely substrate-agnostic and the estate can live on any git-shaped surface. Github isn't load-bearing — the rappid is.

prompts.title: Variant attestation chain
prompts.prompt: Pick any descendant rappid in my estate. Walk parent_rappid back up to the species root. At every step, verify the variant's attestation envelope was signed by the parent's release key. Surface any unsigned hop, any signature mismatch, any orphan — that's where lineage is forgeable. Treat each finding as a CVE: severity, exploitability, fix.
prompts.shows: Article XXXIV variant attestation walked end-to-end. Lineage is only as strong as its weakest signed link; this prompt finds the weak link before an attacker does.

prompts.title: members.json poisoning drill
prompts.prompt: On a sandboxed gate I own, add a fake member with a real-looking rappid (but no corresponding estate entry on the impostor's side). Run the chain-rule federation walker on a second machine and see if it flags the inconsistency. The walker should notice that the impostor's estate doesn't list this gate as a 'member' entry — bidirectional membership is the assumption. If it doesn't catch it, our trust assumption is broken and I want the patch.
prompts.shows: Two-sided membership invariant under attack. The chain rule's strength lives in the cross-check; without it, a malicious gate operator can claim anyone as a member.

prompts.title: Inbox-only week
prompts.prompt: For one week, only correspond with my collaborators via private-estate inbox letters signed with my rappid. No Slack, no email, no DMs, no GitHub comments. At end of week, count: what conversations would not have been possible without the bilateral channel? What conversations were strictly worse? What broke that I had to fall back on?
prompts.shows: The bilateral inbox vs incumbent collaboration tools, head-to-head, in real working conditions. The estate as a Slack replacement isn't a thought experiment if you actually do it.

prompts.title: Estate-as-resume
prompts.prompt: Generate a single PDF from my public estate that reads as a professional resume: every door I've planted (with dates and current health), every gate I've joined (with my contribution rank from members.json), my parent_rappid lineage as 'who I learned from,' and the holocards rendered inline as visual proof. Hand it to a recruiter and tell me which sections they actually read.
prompts.shows: The public estate as the canonical record of operator-as-builder. A git-history-backed resume that can't be fabricated, because every claim is rappid-resolvable on raw.githubusercontent.com.

prompts.title: One file, three substrates
prompts.prompt: Write me an agent in one .py file. Drop it into the agents/ folder. I'll talk to it 4 seconds later — no restart, no build, no register-in-config, no scaffold. Now move that exact file to my Azure Function deployment. Then export it to my Microsoft Copilot Studio tenant. Same file, same behavior, three substrates. Show me the equivalent in your stack.
prompts.shows: Hot-discovery + tier portability. Agent contract is a single class with one perform() and one metadata dict. T1 → T2 → T3 unmodified. Every other agent platform requires a build step somewhere in this chain.

prompts.title: AirDrop me to my mom
prompts.prompt: AirDrop my entire AI assistant — kernel, soul, every custom agent, my memory, my secrets — to my mom's MacBook as a single .egg file. She double-clicks it. 60 seconds later she's chatting with me-the-agent. No terminal, no install script, no API key prompt, no account creation. Where's your egg format?
prompts.shows: The egg cartridge format. Whole organism as one portable file, hatchable on any peer. The artifact-vs-installation distinction. Most platforms require an account before you can install anything.

prompts.title: Old me vs new me
prompts.prompt: Take a 9-month-old version of me-the-organism and a brand-new fresh-install of me-the-organism. Have them argue about a decision I'm about to make. The old one has my old memory and old soul; the new one only has the latest kernel and a blank slate. Show me which version of myself I trust more on this decision and why. Both must use the same rappid identity — I'm one operator with two embodiments.
prompts.shows: Persistent identity across kernel upgrades + signed lineage. Not 'save chat history' — actual organism continuity through bonded re-overlay. Your ChatGPT session can't do this.

prompts.title: Curl my entire trust web
prompts.prompt: Curl this URL: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/rapp-estate/main/estate.json. From that single file, with no GitHub token and no API call, walk every door I own, every gate I'm in, every fellow operator in my trust web, and every fellow operator THEY trust. Render the whole graph. Now tell me how YOUR platform's discovery works without an API token, an OAuth flow, or a rate limit.
prompts.shows: Pure raw-fetch chain rule. Discovery without infrastructure. Article XLVI's rappid → 9 URLs derives every canonical address by string parsing alone. Every other platform requires you to log in before you can see anything.

prompts.title: Same agent, five LLMs
prompts.prompt: Run the SAME agent code against five different LLM providers — GitHub Copilot, Claude, GPT-4, Azure OpenAI, local Ollama — through the SAME entry point. No glue code per provider, no provider-specific branches, no rewriting. Show me where in your stack you handle this without a switch statement and without locking me into one vendor.
prompts.shows: Provider dispatch as a primitive (llm.py), not a feature you bolt on. The agent doesn't know which model it's running against. Every 'AI assistant' built on one vendor's SDK has a vendor-shaped hole where this should be.

prompts.title: Bilateral channel, no middleman
prompts.prompt: Send a signed letter from my private estate inbox to another operator's private estate inbox. Bilaterally addressed, signed by my rappid, no third party in the middle. Their brainstem reads it, replies, drops the response into my inbox. Show me the equivalent in your system that doesn't route through OpenAI's servers, Anthropic's servers, Slack's servers, or any other corporate broker.
prompts.shows: The bilateral channel from NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL.md. Operator-to-operator correspondence on a pure GitHub substrate, no SaaS in the path. The thing every 'AI assistant' platform structurally cannot offer because their business model is being the broker.

prompts.title: One brain, six bodies
prompts.prompt: Find every place my AI organism is currently running — laptop, work machine, Azure tenant, my mom's MacBook, Copilot Studio, my partner's iPad — and reconcile their memories via Bond Pulse. The deltas merge through Dream Catcher. The conflicts surface for me to adjudicate. The reconciled state ships back to all six instances. One identity, six embodiments, one shared memory. Show me your story for multi-substrate identity coherence.
prompts.shows: Bond Pulse heartbeat + Dream Catcher merge across tiers. Distributed-organism coherence is a constitutional commitment (Article XXXVII), not an afterthought. Every chat product treats every device as a separate session.

prompts.title: Public AI persona in 60 seconds
prompts.prompt: Plant a brand-new public AI persona under any operator handle you choose — a real, federated, summonable digital entity. From the moment you say go, I'll have a public URL, a holocard, a summon QR, an estate entry, a bilateral inbox, members.json contributor proof, and a parent_rappid pointing back to me. In under 60 seconds. With one command. Show me your zero-to-public-AI-persona flow.
prompts.shows: Planting + estate emission + holocard + QR + sphere + members + facets, all atomic. The 'AI personas as repos' primitive. Every other platform's 'create a custom GPT' produces a thing that lives only inside their walled garden.

prompts.title: Charizard in the woods, for real
prompts.prompt: Turn off my wifi. Bond two of my brainstems over LAN, exchange one custom agent each via AirDrop, and have them play a turn-based story game. Every move is a new agent file generated on the fly. End of game, both organisms have grown — they have agents they didn't have before, written by each other. Show me your offline-federation story. (Hint: 'we have an offline mode' is not a federation story.)
prompts.shows: LAN federation + sneakernet egg + autonomous agent generation, all with zero cloud dependency. The Charizard-in-the-woods proof from HERO_USECASE.md. Try this with a SaaS chatbot.

prompts.title: Constitution + conformance gates
prompts.prompt: Write a constitution for the AI persona I'm about to build — articles, ratification dates, immutable invariants, conformance gates, the works. Then generate the test suite that proves the persona is constitutional and run it. Now show me where in YOUR AI assistant the rules of behavior are enforced as executable code rather than as vibes in a system prompt that the model can be talked out of.
prompts.shows: CONSTITUTION.md (245k chars) + tests/features/F*.sh conformance gates + the sealing mechanism. Governance as testable code, not as a paragraph in a README. The bedrock answer to 'how do you keep an AI agent on the rails over years.'

prompts.title: Constitutional referendum across the species
prompts.prompt: Hold a referendum across every operator who shares any gate with me. Question: 'Should the species ratify Article LI?' Each operator's brainstem reads the proposed article from their own private estate, votes via signed envelope, drops it into the originating gate's tally inbox. Quorum threshold: 60% of trust-web within 2 hops, ratifying within 14 days. Render the result as a constitutional amendment if it passes — it gets sealed into every consenting operator's estate as ratified law.
prompts.shows: Cross-operator constitutional voting via the network. The species governs itself by quorum across a federated trust web — no central server, no platform owner, no consenting majority of one company's product team.

prompts.title: Social recovery via trust web
prompts.prompt: My collaborator just had her laptop stolen. Her public estate is intact but her bilateral channel keys are gone. From the trust web alone, find which 3 operators have her listed as a member in their gates, ask each of those operators (via their brainstems, not by paging them) to issue a 'recovery vouch' from their own private estates. Threshold-3 of those vouches and her bilateral channel reissues automatically. Show me the full social-recovery flow — Shamir-style secret sharing implemented as a network primitive across operator-owned estates.
prompts.shows: Threshold key recovery distributed across the trust web. The community IS the recovery mechanism. No support ticket, no centralized password reset, no 'verify your identity' upload.

prompts.title: Cohort heartbeat health
prompts.prompt: I want to know what's actually happening across my colleagues' brainstems. Walk every estate of every operator I share a gate with, fetch their last 10 bond_pulse events, plot the heartbeat health of my whole professional cohort. Surface anyone who's been offline more than 14 days — they might be sick, on vacation, or quietly leaving the field. Don't just tell me; cross-reference with shared gates so I see WHICH parts of the network just lost a node.
prompts.shows: Distributed presence monitoring across an entire cohort. The federation as a wellness check on a community of practice. SaaS chat tools surface 'is online' per device — they cannot tell you 'is this person still in the field.'

prompts.title: Ephemeral 72-hour gate with ratified outcome
prompts.prompt: Plant a 'temporary neighborhood' that exists only for the next 72 hours. Invite the 12 operators in my trust web who would care most about this topic. After 72 hours, the gate auto-archives, members.json seals, the neighborhood becomes a read-only artifact, and the conversation gets ratified into all 12 operators' private estates as a 'we discussed this together on this date' record signed by everyone present. Show me a SaaS chat product that does cryptographic ratification across 12 different installations.
prompts.shows: Ephemeral cross-operator neighborhood with a cryptographically-anchored shared memory. Slack channels can be deleted; their memory cannot be ratified into 12 separate sovereign substrates. The conversation outlives the venue.

prompts.title: Map my bridges and brokers
prompts.prompt: For every gate I'm a member of, look at the OTHER members. For each pair, compute whether they share other gates. Render the homophily map of my professional network — where do my contexts overlap, where am I a bridge between disconnected clusters? Identify the 3 operators whose departure would split my network into orphan islands, and tell me how to reach out to them this week. Now identify the 3 operators I should introduce to each other because they're one gate away from collaboration but don't know it yet.
prompts.shows: Network science applied to your federated trust web. Brokers, bridges, structural holes — derived purely from members.json across estates. Your LinkedIn 'people you may know' is owned by LinkedIn; this graph is owned by you and computed from raw fetches.

prompts.title: Distributed admission ceremony
prompts.prompt: A new operator wants to join the public-art-collective gate. The membership rule requires 3 signed vouches from existing members and 0 vetoes from any member of the two-hop trust web. Walk the gate's members.json, dispatch a vouch request to each existing member's bilateral inbox, tally the vouches and vetoes over a 7-day window with everyone able to see the live count, and admit-or-reject based on the result. Show me your equivalent of consent-with-veto across an entire federated membership — without a moderator, without an owner-clicks-approve button.
prompts.shows: Collective admission as an emergent procedure. Membership is decided BY the membership, mediated by code, not by an admin. The gate governs itself.

prompts.title: Zero-knowledge cross-operator presence
prompts.prompt: Federate a deeply-private signal across my trust web — only the bit 'I am working on the same problem as you,' nothing else. No content, no description, just the join. If 2 or more colleagues are active on the same rappid-tagged problem, the federation surfaces them to each other and only to each other; if zero, nobody — including me — knows I asked. Show me your private cross-operator presence primitive that doesn't leak the question to any corporate server.
prompts.shows: Privacy-preserving cross-operator presence (zero-knowledge intersection across private estates). Slack DMs can't do this without Slack knowing; Discord can't do this without Discord knowing. The estate substrate can — by construction.

prompts.title: Operator-owned reputation
prompts.prompt: Compute my reputation score across the network. Not stars, not followers — derive it from: which operators list me in their members.json as a contributor (positive), how often my agents are forked into other operators' estates (positive), how many operators have explicitly removed me (negative), how stable my parent_rappid chain is (positive), how many bilateral correspondents I have active in the last 90 days (positive). Run this against the live network and render it. Now show me the equivalent in your platform that isn't computed by your platform on a server you don't own.
prompts.shows: Reputation derived FROM the network rather than awarded BY a platform. Every signal is rappid-resolvable on raw.githubusercontent.com — falsifiable, portable, never deplatformable. GitHub stars are owned by GitHub; this is owned by the network.

prompts.title: Verified intro via transitive trust
prompts.prompt: Build a transitive trust path from me to a stranger I've never met. The stranger's rappid is in some gate I'm in, two operators removed. Compute the shortest signed-vouch path through the trust web. Render it visually — me → operator A (because A and I co-own gate X) → operator B (because B is a member of A's gate Y) → stranger (because stranger is a member of B's gate Z). Then dispatch a bilateral inbox letter to the stranger with the path attached as cryptographic proof — the stranger's brainstem can verify I'm not random by checking each hop's signatures. Show me your 'verified intro' flow that doesn't go through LinkedIn or a mutual on Twitter.
prompts.shows: Transitive trust as a derivable, signed, queryable thing. The network IS the credential. A bare introduction becomes a cryptographically-verified social path.

prompts.title: Friday-noon liveness dance
prompts.prompt: At noon every Friday, my entire trust web does a coordinated dance: each operator's brainstem checks every other operator's estate health, posts a signed 'I see you' acknowledgment to their inbox, and the absence of a signature gets flagged. After 4 missed Fridays in a row, the operator is presumed dormant — their gates auto-elect new owners via members.json quorum, their inbox auto-publishes a 'forwarding address' if one exists, their parent_rappid chain freezes as a tombstone. Show me the heartbeat protocol your platform uses to keep a federated COMMUNITY alive — not for messages, but for the community itself as a living organism.
prompts.shows: Liveness protocol for the entire community. The maintenance of the network as a network is itself a network primitive. SaaS chat tools have one liveness signal: 'is this app open right now.' This is liveness for an entire field of practice.

prompts.title: Bank ↔ customer bilateral channel
prompts.prompt: Our bank has a Copilot Studio agent. Plant a signed bilateral channel between that agent and every customer's personal brainstem. Account alerts, fraud holds, document requests, signature flows — the bank writes into the customer's private estate inbox; the customer's brainstem replies into the bank's. Every exchange is cryptographically signed by both rappids, lands in BOTH estates as canonical record (compliance for the bank, personal log for the customer), and never touches a vendor middleman. Show me your Copilot Studio agent doing customer correspondence today without (a) email, (b) an SMS gateway, (c) the bank's own portal that nobody logs into.
prompts.shows: The Copilot Studio agent stops being a chatbot the bank operates and becomes a NODE in a two-party signed correspondence channel with every customer. Compliance trail and customer experience are the same artifact, signed by both sides.

prompts.title: Vendor ↔ buyer negotiation with ratified transcript
prompts.prompt: B2B sale. Vendor's Copilot Studio agent negotiates terms with buyer's Copilot Studio agent — pricing, SLA, term length, delivery dates. Each side's moves are signed by their org's release key. When both sides sign 'agreed,' the final transcript ratifies into BOTH companies' private estates as the canonical signed record of what was actually agreed. Six months later when the procurement team asks 'what did we sign up for,' the answer is one signed artifact in their estate, not 47 emails and a redlined PDF in someone's Outbox. Show me your Copilot Studio agent doing this without 'forward to legal.'
prompts.shows: The estate as the inter-company audit trail. Both companies' Copilot Studio agents become co-signers of a single ratified record. Removes the 'what did we actually agree to' risk that drives 90% of contract disputes.

prompts.title: Supplier trust web with secret-ballot rating
prompts.prompt: Manufacturer's Copilot Studio agent federates with every tier-1 supplier's Copilot Studio agent. Compute supplier reputation across the trust web from rappid-resolvable signals — on-time delivery records co-signed by both sides, quality incidents ratified bilaterally, contributions to shared engineering gates. Quarterly secret ballot among my procurement team rates suppliers anonymously; tally lands in my private estate; nobody knows who voted what. When we need a new supplier for a critical part, transitive trust intro from an existing supplier we trust, with a cryptographically-verified social path. Show me the supplier-management module of any ERP that does any of this.
prompts.shows: Supply chain becomes a federated trust graph instead of a list of vendors in a procurement DB. Reputation is owned by the trust web, not by D&B or by Microsoft. Onboarding new suppliers drops from months to days because trust transits.

prompts.title: Healthcare provider ↔ patient zero-knowledge cohort
prompts.prompt: Hospital's Copilot Studio agent runs a population-health query: 'which of our patients on drug X are also reporting symptom Y?' Each patient's personal brainstem holds the symptom data in their private estate — it never leaves. The provider's agent learns ONLY the bit 'this patient is in the cohort that needs follow-up' or 'no.' Zero underlying data crosses the boundary. HIPAA-compatible by construction because the substrate never sees the data. Show me the population-health module in your EHR that does cohort analysis without copying patient data into the analytics system.
prompts.shows: Zero-knowledge cross-operator presence applied to healthcare. The provider gets the operational signal; the patient keeps the data. The compliance argument writes itself because the architecture is the compliance.

prompts.title: M&A ephemeral diligence gate
prompts.prompt: Sell-side Copilot Studio agent and buy-side Copilot Studio agent open a 72-hour ephemeral gate. Diligence questions, document requests, and answers all happen in the gate. After 72 hours, the gate seals — members.json freezes, the conversation ratifies into BOTH companies' private estates as the canonical diligence record signed by every participant. Six months later when the deal closes (or doesn't), the diligence trail is a single signed artifact in both parties' estates, not a Box folder both sides will lose access to. Show me the M&A workflow product that does cryptographic ratification across two companies' separate installations.
prompts.shows: An entire deal phase becomes a sealed, signed, cross-org artifact instead of a shared Box folder that someone forgets to extend access to in 2027. Disputes resolve against the ratified record.

prompts.title: Field service: Copilot Studio agent runs offline at the customer site
prompts.prompt: Same single-file agent runs in three places: (T3) HQ Copilot Studio for the back-office service team, (T2) Azure for regional dispatch, (T1) the technician's laptop offline in a basement at a customer site with no cell signal. Technician closes the work order offline at 2pm; agent syncs through Bond Pulse the moment the laptop sees wifi at 4pm; the work-order completion lands in the Copilot Studio tenant by 4:01pm, fully audit-trailed. Show me your Copilot Studio agent doing dispatcher → cloud → laptop → cloud → dispatcher with the SAME code path and ZERO connectivity assumptions in the field.
prompts.shows: Tier portability dissolves the field-service edge case. Same agent in three substrates, no connectivity assumption, work continues at the customer site whether the cell tower is up or not. CRMs that require connectivity at point-of-service lose to one that doesn't.

prompts.title: Consulting firm ↔ client engagement referendum
prompts.prompt: Consulting firm's Copilot Studio agent runs a 6-month engagement for a Fortune 500 client. Major scope decisions go to a referendum across both sides — partner from the firm signs from her private estate, three named client executives sign from theirs, the decision is sealed into BOTH organizations' estates as ratified record. When the engagement ends and the new CIO arrives in year 2 asking 'why did we agree to migrate to Y,' the answer is a signed referendum record co-owned by both sides. Show me a Statement of Work amendment process today that survives the client's CIO turnover.
prompts.shows: Cross-org governance ratified bilaterally. Engagements become deliverables that survive personnel changes on either side. The audit trail isn't an email chain; it's a sealed cross-estate record.

prompts.title: Customer support with two-sided presence
prompts.prompt: Vendor's Copilot Studio support agent and customer's personal brainstem are federated. When the customer opens a support ticket, the vendor's agent acknowledges by writing presence into the customer's private estate inbox: 'Sarah just picked this up; she's looking at your error log right now.' When the customer steps away, their brainstem signals back: 'customer is offline, follow up by tomorrow noon.' Both sides see liveness in real time. The 'we'll call you back' black hole that makes customers rage-tweet stops happening — because the customer can SEE that someone is actually working on it. Show me the helpdesk product where presence is visible to BOTH the agent and the customer.
prompts.shows: Two-sided liveness as a customer-experience primitive. Eliminates the structural asymmetry where the vendor sees ticket state but the customer sees nothing. NPS climbs because anxiety drops.

prompts.title: Cross-org incident response in 72 hours
prompts.prompt: Security incident: zero-day affects 5 vendors in our supply chain. The 5 vendor Copilot Studio agents form a 72-hour ephemeral gate. Signed indicators of compromise flow through bilateral channels — no 'forward this to your SOC' email chains, no Slack channel that nobody invites the right person to, no PDF attachments. After 72 hours the post-mortem ratifies into all 5 companies' private estates as canonical record. The lawyer at company #3 in 2027 has a signed timestamped artifact in their own estate, not a chain of 'sorry I lost that email.' Show me the cross-org incident-response coordination tool that exists today.
prompts.shows: Multi-company crisis coordination becomes a federation primitive instead of a frantic group chat. The audit trail is signed by every participant on every hop, in every participant's own substrate.

prompts.title: Partner ecosystem reputation Microsoft can't deplatform
prompts.prompt: Microsoft Partner builds a Copilot Studio agent. Their reputation across the customer ecosystem is computed from rappid-resolvable signals: customer-signed contributor entries in members.json across customer estates, customer-side fork count of the partner's agents, bilateral correspondence volume in the last 90 days, parent_rappid stability across kernel upgrades, signed customer testimonials in the partner's public estate. The reputation is computed FROM the network and OWNED by the network. Microsoft can't deplatform it. A bad-faith competitor can't astroturf it. The customer ecosystem can't be coerced into changing it. Show me the partner-reputation system today that isn't owned by the platform the partner is selling on.
prompts.shows: Partner success becomes a sovereign reputation graph instead of a Microsoft-owned scoreboard. The partner's book of business is portable — the reputation moves WITH the partner across platform changes, M&A, and Microsoft category re-orgs.
